form_analysis.form_header_text: Congressman Juan Ciscomani FY2027 Appropriations Programmatic and Language Request Form
form_analysis.form_instructions_text: This form is based on FY2027 Appropriations guidance provided by the House Appropriations Committee. After submission, if there are changes or updates to the information, please advise the staff responsible for the changes. Please be advised that staff may reach out with additional questions should the Appropriations Committee require additional information on a submission, both before and after the submission deadline.

fields.label:
All Defense Line Number and Program Element (PE)
Please tell us the line number in the PBR, Program Element, or program name if requesting language.�
fields.rationale: The request is for the Science Mission Directorate (SMD) topline account under NASA's Science budget. This program name is the standard identifier for programmatic funding requests within the Science account.

sections.description: Information regarding the funding or language being requested.

fields.label:
Please identify the type of request:

NOTE: Each request requires an individual form, if you have multiple requests please fill out a separate form for each - this includes programmatic requests with language associated.�
fields.rationale: The request context specifies this as a programmatic funding request for the Science Mission Directorate.

fields.label: Amount/policy included specifically for this item in the President's budget request for FY 2027 (if available):
fields.draft_value: * Not available; the President's Budget Request for FY 2027 has not yet been released
fields.rationale: The FY 2027 President's Budget Request is currently unknown as noted in the reference values.

fields.label:
Description of program request:
Please give a brief justification for your request and�include�
� � �Agency:��
� � �Account:��
� � �Agency Subdivision:
� � �Program:
� � �Activity:�
fields.draft_value: * Agency: NASA; Account: Science; Subdivision: Science Mission Directorate; Program: SMD * $9 billion request to restore mission stability and scientific momentum following FY 2026 disruptions * Funds Decadal Survey priorities across planetary science, heliophysics, and astrophysics * Ensures critical U.S. space leadership and retention of the specialized technical workforce
fields.rationale: Draws on programmatic data and advocacy talking points regarding workforce stability and Decadal Survey priorities.

fields.label: Funding request for FY 2027:
options: Support the President's FY2026 budget request
fields.rationale: The $9 billion ask is a specific funding level requested by The Planetary Society and will be explained in the narrative justification.

fields.label:
Federal funding history:
E.g. $100,000 in FY24, $150,000 in FY25 Please include the complete funding history.
fields.draft_value: * FY 2025: $7.566 billion (PBR); $7.334 billion (Enacted) * FY 2026: $3.908 billion (PBR); $7.250 billion (Enacted)
fields.rationale: Uses authoritative SMD topline funding history from reference data.

fields.label: If requesting a funding increase, please suggest an appropriate offset from this bill:
fields.rationale: Specific offsets are not identified in the provided context and are typically determined by congressional staff.

Description of Language Request:
Please give a brief justification for your request.

fields.label: Are you making this request to other offices? If yes, please list all offices receiving this request:
fields.draft_value:
* Reps. Don Bacon (R-NE) and Judy Chu (D-CA) are leading an existing multi-member House letter in support of this request.
* Request also being submitted to additional House offices.
fields.rationale: Request is being submitted to multiple offices; Bacon/Chu lead the House letter per reference values.

fields.label: Of the requests made by this group in this bill, this request ranks as priority [ ] of [ ] total requests:
fields.rationale: Priority ranking is specific to the individual submitter's internal strategy and cannot be determined from the provided programmatic context.

fields.label: Why is the request a good use of taxpayer dollars? �Please fully state your justification for use of federal taxpayer dollars:
fields.draft_value: * Economic Return: Every $1 invested in NASA returns approximately $3 to the U.S. economy * Strategic Advantage: Maintains U.S. leadership against China's rapid expansion in space science exploration * Workforce Development: Inspires the STEM talent pipeline and supports high-tech domestic manufacturing * Scientific Value: Supports rigorous, peer-reviewed roadmaps prioritized by the National Academies
fields.rationale: Woven from strategic advocacy talking points regarding economic impact, competition, and scientific merit.

fields.label: Impact on Arizona's 6th Congressional District and other comments/miscellaneous information: �
fields.draft_value: * Direct Investment: Over $4.48 million in NASA Science obligations within AZ-06 from FY 2023 to FY 2025 * Local Industry: $4.03 million active contract for Tucson's Litespar Inc to develop laser transmitters for atmospheric science * Research Excellence: $251k grant for the Planetary Science Institute in Tucson to execute national science priorities * Economic Support: Sustains specialized aerospace and environmental research jobs in the Tucson area
fields.rationale: Directly references local spending data, including specific recipient names (Litespar Inc, Planetary Science Institute) and dollar amounts for the 6th district.
